channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vera C. Rubin Observatory Launches Decade-Long Cosmic Survey Using Largest Digital Camera
item.description: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile has officially begun one of the most ambitious astronomical projects in history: a decade-long survey of the southern sky using the largest digital camera ever c...

item.title: California’s Early History and Influence Before U.S. Independence
item.description: This opinion column by Patt Morrison examines California’s early historical development and argues that the region was already undergoing major social, political, and cultural transformations at the s...

item.title: MILF leader calls on BARMM local officials to ensure free and fair voting in historic parliamentary elections
item.description: Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) first vice chairperson Mohagher Iqbal has called on governors, mayors, and other local chief executives in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BAR...

item.title: Former Downing Street aide says Labour was unprepared for government and overly pessimistic about economy
item.description: Morgan McSweeney, former chief of staff to Keir Starmer, has acknowledged that the Labour Party was not sufficiently prepared for governing before entering office after the 2024 general election. Spea...

item.title: Legal expert says Sara Duterte impeachment case could be brought before the Supreme Court
item.description: Former Supreme Court Associate Justice Jose C. Azcuna has raised the possibility that the impeachment proceedings involving Vice President Sara Duterte could eventually be brought before the Supreme C...

item.title: Paola Pitagora and Her Legacy in 1960s–70s Italian Cinema
item.description: Paola Pitagora, born in 1941, is an Italian actress widely recognized for her strong emotional presence and expressive performances during the 1960s and 1970s. She became a notable figure in postwar I...

item.title: Todd Blanche Speech to National Guard Disrupted by Protesters in Washington, D.C.
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche delivered a public address to National Guard members stationed in Washington, D.C., where he aimed to thank them for their service and their role in supporting sec...

item.title: Philippines Reaches Upper Middle Income Status, Marcos Cites Economic Gains Amid Inflation Concerns
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. welcomed the World Bank’s classification of the Philippines as an upper middle income country, describing it as a strong vote of confidence in the country’s economic dir...

item.title: DML 002 announce upcoming Afro-pop collaboration with Scar Mkadinali on new single 'Kizombi'
item.description: Emerging Kenyan Afro-pop duo DML 002, made up of Whity 002 and Dierxy 002 from Kwale County along the coast, is preparing to release a new single titled 'Kizombi' featuring rapper Scar Mkadinali from ...

item.title: Two people seriously injured after stabbing in Scarborough; police search for suspects
item.description: Two people were seriously injured following a stabbing incident in Scarborough on Wednesday night, according to Toronto police. Officers were called to the area of Nugget Avenue and McCowan Road aroun...

item.title: Driver of CHADEMA Deputy Chairperson Found Dead in Kigoma Guest House as Police Investigate
item.description: A driver working for CHADEMA Vice Chairperson John Heche, identified as Suez Daniel Maradufu, has been found dead in a guest house room in Kigoma while on an official trip. The incident happened on th...

item.title: Princess of Wales attends Wimbledon in royal blue Gabriela Hearst suit and supports community initiatives
item.description: The Princess of Wales made a notable public appearance at Wimbledon, attending the Championships for the first time this year. She arrived at SW19 wearing a tailored royal blue suit by Gabriela Hearst...

item.title: Kasabian Finsbury Park 2026 stage times revealed
item.description: Kasabian are set to headline a major outdoor concert at London’s Finsbury Park on Saturday 4 July, with organisers Festival Republic confirming the full stage schedule ahead of the event. The show for...

item.title: High Court Allows Ruto to Reappoint Former Cabinet Secretaries but Orders Gender Balance Fix
item.description: The High Court in Kenya has delivered a split ruling allowing President William Ruto to reappoint Cabinet Secretaries who had earlier been dismissed during the 2024 Cabinet shake-up. The majority deci...

item.title: SHC orders inquiry report on alleged HIV outbreak at Karachi SESSI hospital linked to syringe reuse
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC) has sought a detailed report from the Sindh health secretary and the provincial police chief after a public interest petition was filed regarding an alleged HIV outbreak at ...

item.title: Foreign-Owned Businesses Looted During Protests in Parts of Durban
item.description: On 30 June 2026, foreign-owned shops in several Durban areas faced significant looting amid demonstrations, resulting in heavy financial losses and property damage. Affected communities included Maria...

item.title: Kenyan Scientist Samuel Oyola Secures Gates Funding to Advance AI-Based Disease Surveillance
item.description: A Kenyan scientist, Samuel Oyola, has received a KSh 187 million ($1.45 million) grant from the Gates Foundation to develop an AI-powered public health platform aimed at improving disease detection an...

item.title: Biopic Film 'Edjop' About Martial Law Activist Edgar Jopson Set for September Cinema Release
item.description: The much-awaited Filipino film 'Edjop' is finally heading to cinemas nationwide this September 2026. This biopic tells the inspiring and poignant story of Edgar 'Edjop' Jopson, a prominent student act...

item.title: Call for greater competition and consumer-focused reform in Canada’s banking sector
item.description: The article argues that while Canada’s banking system has historically provided stability and resilience through global financial crises, it must now evolve to better serve Canadians facing rising cos...

item.title: Barrick recognised as Tanzania’s top taxpayer at Presidential awards ceremony
item.description: Barrick Mining Corporation has been named Tanzania’s leading taxpayer for the 2024/25 financial year, receiving top recognition at the Presidential Top Taxpayers Awards gala held in Dar es Salaam. The...
